DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF TRIIO, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL THIRD PARTY DEPONENTS TO PRODUCE BUSINESS RECORDS PURSUANT TO DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS- THE MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHALL PROVIDE NECESSARY CREDENTIALS UPON REQUEST TO ENABLE WITNESSES TO ACCESS DATA AND PRODUCE RESPONSIVE INFORMATION. THIRD PARTY WITNESSES SHALL TAKE REASONABLE STEPS TO PRESERVE ALL INFORMATION UNTIL DISPOSITION OF LITIGATION. IF ANY INFORMATION IS WITHHELD ON PRIVILEGE GROUNDS OR OTHERWISE, THEN A SUFFICIENTLY DETAILED PRIVILEGE LOG MUST BE PROVIDED WITH THE RESPONSES. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO USE A THIRD PARTY VENDOR TO CONDUCT THE SEARCH AND PRODUCE THE RECORDS MERELY TO "RULE OUT THE POSSIBILITY OF SPOLIATION" IS UNJUSTIFIED AT THIS TIME AND THEREFORE DENIED. THE WITNESSES SHALL PRODUCE RESPONSIVE INFORMATION TO DEPOSITION OFFICER LISTED IN THE SUBPOENAS ON OR BEFORE MAY 30, 2016, ALONG WITH A CUSTODIAN OF RECORDS DECLARATION PER EVIDENCE CODE 1561. EACH PARTY'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: AARON MINNIS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF TRIIO, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set One, Form Interrogatories Set One, Request For Admissions Set One, Request For Production Set One And For Monetary Sanction - Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to special and form interrogatories, requests for admissions, and production of documents, is GRANTED, except as to form interrogatories nos. 14.1 and 14.2. Defendant's boiler-plate objections and evasive answers are improper; each answer must be as complete and straight-forward as the information available to the responding party permits. If an interrogatory cannot be answered completely, then it should be answered to the extent possible. Defendant Vu Nguyen shall serve plaintiff with Code-compliant and verified responses and documents on or before May 30, 2016. Defendant may redact only to the extent necessary to protect private or financial information. Any documents withheld on privilege grounds should be noted in a sufficiently detailed privilege log. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro tem: Aaron Minnis, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not Reported. =302/jpt

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF TRIIO, LLC's Motion To Compel Third Party Deponents To Produce Business Records Pursuant To Deposition Subpoenas And Request For Sanctions- The motion to compel production is GRANTED. Plaintiff shall provide necessary credentials upon request to enable witnesses to access data and produce responsive information. Third party witnesses shall take reasonable steps to preserve all information until disposition of litigation. If any information is withheld on privilege grounds or otherwise, then a sufficiently detailed privilege log must be provided with the responses. Plaintiff's request to use a third party vendor to conduct the search and produce the records merely to "rule out the possibility of spoliation" is unjustified at this time and therefore DENIED. The witnesses shall produce responsive information to deposition officer listed in the subpoenas on or before May 30, 2016, along with a custodian of records declaration per Evidence Code 1561. Each party's request for sanctions is DENIED. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro tem: Aaron Minnis, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not Reported. =302/jpt

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT VU NGUYEN'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, Defendant's demurrer to the second and seventh causes of action is overruled as to both claims and defendant's motion to strike is denied as to punitive damages and granted with twenty days leave to amend as to attorney's fees. The bad faith and fraud claims are adequately pled. The bad faith claim alleges that defendant committed conduct that was not explicitly proscribed by the contract but contravened the implied term of good faith. Though labeled "intentional misrepresentation," the fraud claims alleges a cognizable claim for promissory fraud. The fraud allegations are sufficient to support punitive damages. Plaintiff has not alleged any basis for attorney's fees, but is given leave to do so, if it can do so in good faith. SINGLE ORDER SIGNED RE: BOTH DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/HK)
